scenarios.execution_notes:
Failed: Timeout Error. Element is missing or has not been loaded due to perfomance issue.

Failed: AUTH-FPW-001 – Known bug: "Forgot password?" link has href="#" and does not redirect to a password reset page. No recovery flow is triggered.

DEFECT AUTH-FPW-001: The 'Forgot password?' link has href='#' and performs no navigation. No password reset flow is triggered. Test marked test.fail() with defect label and severity blocker.

scenarios.title: Importing XLSX with valid delegation data updates requirement delegation details

Open the file; on the `Delegation Details` tab, populate: `Delegated to Product ID` = a known PICASso product ID (format PIC-xxx), `Delegated to Release ID` = a known release ID, `Delegation Due Date` = a future date in YYYY-MM-DD format
Return to the Process Requirements tab and click the import/upload button and upload the modified XLSX
Navigate to the `View/Edit` popup of the process requirement specified in the import
expected_results: The Process Requirements grid is visible
A `.xlsx` file is downloaded
The file is updated and saved

scenarios.title: Importing XLSX where Product ID exists in PICASso links the product and displays its name

expected_results: The import completes without errors

scenarios.title: Importing XLSX with both Product ID and Product Name empty returns an error

Upload the XLSX via the import control on the Process Requirements tab

expected_results: The file is ready for upload

The requirement retains its previous delegation values (or no delegation if none existed)

scenarios.title: Importing XLSX with Delegated Requirement Code not in the requirements library returns an error
steps: Prepare the XLSX with a `Delegated to Requirement Code` = `NONEXISTENT-REQ-999` (a code that does not exist)
Upload the XLSX via the import control on the Process Requirements tab
Verify the affected requirement's delegation details are not updated
expected_results: The file is ready
The import returns an error result; an error message is visible indicating the requirement code does not exist in the library
The requirement retains its previous delegation values

scenarios.title: Importing XLSX with Delegated Requirement Code not scoped for the specified release returns an error
steps: Prepare the XLSX with a valid `Delegated to Product ID` and `Delegated to Release ID` but a `Delegated to Requirement Code` that is not in scope for that release
Upload the XLSX via the import control on the Process Requirements tab
expected_results: The file is ready
The import returns an error result; an error message indicates the requirement code is not scoped for the specified release

scenarios.title: Importing XLSX with a past Delegation Due Date succeeds without error

expected_results: The file is ready

scenarios.title: Controls with open findings: correct display and action options in Actions Closure
scenarios.description: PIC-7879: Open findings display and available actions during closure
steps:
Step 1: # Set up or locate a DOC in pre-Action Closure status with  At least one Control with an open finding
# Move the DOC to Action Closure status in the Actions Closure stage
# Navigate to the Controls tab
# Check the status of each Control

Step 2: # Log in as a user with EVALUATE_ITS_CONTROLS_RISKS
# Open a DOC in Action Closure with Controls in Evidence Required status
# View the Risk Level field for a control in that status
# Attempt to edit it

Step 3: 1)Log in as a user with CREATE_UPDATE_FINDINGS

2)Open a DOC in Action Closure

3)Navigate to a Control in Evidence Required status

4)Locate the Findings section

5)Attempt to:

* Add a new finding
* Edit an existing one

* Risk Level is not editable

scenarios.title: Actions Closure permissions and restrictions per role enforced correctly
scenarios.description: PIC-7885: Role-based access control during Actions Closure stage
steps:
Step 1: 1)Login as a user with appropriate privileges (e.g., CREATE_UPDATE_ACTIONS)

2)Go to the Action Plan or related section for a control or finding

3)Locate a finding or action that needs evidence

4)Enter:

# A valid evidence link
# A comment

5) Submit/Save

Step 2: # Navigate to the Controls or Findings tab
# View findings with status Open
# Click to add action to an open finding
#  Enter details and submit

Step 3: 1) Go to the Action Plan tab

2)View existing actions (status: In Progress, Completed, etc.)

3)Select an action to:

* Update details (e.g., change owner, update comment)

* Change status (e.g., mark as complete)

Step 4: # Navigate to the relevant Controls tab
# Find an eligible control where actions are complete
# Click Request Risk Re-Assessment
# Confirm request and submit

Step 5: # Go to Controls tab
#  Try to click Add New Control

Step 6: # Navigate to Controls list
# Select an existing control
# Try to use a descope option (UI toggle, menu, checkbox)
# Attempt to save or submit
expected_results:
# Evidence link and comment are successfully saved and visible.
# No error or restriction message is shown

*  Open findings are visible
* User can add a new action for the finding successfully

Existing actions are listed

User is allowed to manage (edit, close) them per privileges

* Re-assessment request is submitted successfully
* Control status changes to something like "Re-Assessment Requested"
New controls can not be added
Existing controls can not be descoped

scenarios.title: Prevent removal of findings from action when control is in Assessment Completed status
scenarios.description: PIC-8232: Finding removal blocked for completed assessments
steps: Step 1: Log in as a user with CREATE_UPDATE_DOC_ACTIONS privilege.
Step 2: Navigate to an Action Plan tab for a Control currently in Assessment Completed status.
Step 3: Open the Findings of Action pop-up for any Action under this Control.
Step 4: Attempt to remove/unlink any Finding from the Action in the pop-up.
Step 5: Change the Control status to something other than Assessment Completed (e.g., "In Progress").
Step 6: Open the Findings of Action pop-up again and attempt to remove a Finding from an Action.
Step 7: Log in as a user without CREATE_UPDATE_DOC_ACTIONS privilege and try to remove a Finding (regardless of Control status).
expected_results: User logs in successfully.
The Action Plan tab loads with related Actions and Findings
The pop-up displays the list of Findings linked to the Action
Removal option/button is disabled or not visible; user cannot remove Finding
Control status updates successfully.
Removal option/button is enabled; user can successfully remove the Finding.
User cannot remove Findings; removal option is unavailable or restricted.

Observe the Org level 1 filter.

Try to modify the filter.

Review the table content

Step 3: Log in as User C.

Open DOC Actions report.

Observe the Org level 2 filter.

Try to modify the filter.

Review the table content

Observe filter availability.

Inspect visible data.

Filters are not restricted.

Table displays all data across all organizations.

No data from other Org level 1 organizations appears.

Table displays only data related to the selected allowed organizations.example (china hub)

No data from any other Org level 2 appears.
No data outside assigned organizations is displayed.

- Control Details Page

"Add Finding" button is *not visible*

scenarios.title: Verify Control re-assessment and role-based editing after actions closure
scenarios.description: PIC-7018: Control re-assessment workflow with correct role permissions
steps: Step 1: Verify User with {{EVALUATE_ITS_CONTROLS_RISKS}} or {{CREATE_UPDATE_FINDINGS}} opens a Control after it’s submitted for re-assessment.
Step 2: User with {{EVALUATE_ITS_CONTROLS_RISKS}} submits the Control back with a *request for clarification* to the DO team.
Step 3: User with {{CREATE_UPDATE_FINDINGS}} adds a *new Finding* to the Control.
Step 4: User with {{CREATE_UPDATE_FINDINGS}} edits the status of an existing Finding by clicking the *Edit* button to close it or change status.
Step 5: User with {{EVALUATE_ITS_CONTROLS_RISKS}} re-evaluates and updates the *Risk Level* of the Control.
expected_results: Control opens in *edit mode* with all previously available editable fields and functionality.
Control is successfully submitted with a clarification request
New Finding is added and visible in the Control.
Finding’s status is updated successfully.
Risk Level is updated and saved correctly.

scenarios.title: Scope Rework triggers email to PO + SM

scenarios.title: Release Completion Reminder triggers email to PO + SM (TO) and SA (CC)

expected_results: An email with subject "Automatic Reminder: Pending action items for Release [RELEASE] for Product [PRODUCT]" is sent

SA is in the CC field

scenarios.title: SA + PQL sign off → Readiness Confirmation email to PO + SM

scenarios.title: Custom Banners Configuration link visible in Notification Configuration
steps: Navigate to BackOffice → Notification Configuration section
Verify the `Custom Banners Configuration` link is listed
Click the `Custom Banners Configuration` link
expected_results: The Notification Configuration section is visible

scenarios.title: Non-admin cannot access Custom Banners Configuration page
steps: Navigate directly to `/GRC_BackOffice/CustomBannerConfig`
expected_results: The page is not accessible; the user is redirected to the landing page or an access-denied screen

scenarios.title: "Create banner" button opens Create Banner popup with all required fields
steps: Navigate to BackOffice → Notification Configuration → `Custom Banners Configuration`
Click the `Create banner` button
Verify popup contains all required fields
expected_results: The Custom Banners Configuration page opens with the empty state message and `Create banner` button
The Create Banner popup opens

The popup contains: `Name` (free text), `Notification Type` dropdown (info/warning), `Start Date and Time` (datetime picker), `End Date and Time` (datetime picker), `Message Text` (SK editor supporting bold/italic/link), `Make Active` checkbox (unchecked by default), `Save` button, `Cancel` button, X close icon

scenarios.title: Creating a banner with all fields filled saves and appears in list
steps: Click the `Create banner` button
Type a unique name (e.g., "Test Info Banner") in the `Name` field
Select `info` from the `Notification Type` dropdown
Set `Start Date and Time` to a future date/time
Set `End Date and Time` to a date/time after the start date
Type a message in the `Message Text` editor
Click `Save`
expected_results: The Create Banner popup opens
The Name field contains the typed text
The `info` option is selected
The Start Date and Time picker shows the selected value
The End Date and Time picker shows the selected value
The editor contains the typed message
The popup closes; the new banner row is visible in the Custom Banners Configuration table with the correct Name, Notification Type, Start/End Date and Time

scenarios.title: Saving Create Banner popup without Name shows validation error
steps: Click the `Create banner` button
Leave the `Name` field empty
Fill all other mandatory fields (Notification Type, Start/End Date, Message Text)
Click `Save`
expected_results: The Create Banner popup opens
The Name field is empty
Other fields are populated
The popup remains open; an inline validation error is shown on the `Name` field

scenarios.title: Saving Create Banner popup without Notification Type shows validation error
steps: Click the `Create banner` button
Fill the `Name` field and other mandatory fields; leave `Notification Type` unselected
Click `Save`
expected_results: The Create Banner popup opens
Notification Type shows default placeholder
The popup remains open; an inline validation error is shown on the `Notification Type` field

scenarios.title: Saving with End Date before Start Date shows validation error
steps: Fill `Name`, `Notification Type`, and `Message Text`
Set `Start Date and Time` to a future date (e.g., tomorrow)
Set `End Date and Time` to a date before the Start Date
Click `Save`
expected_results: Fields are populated
Start Date is set
End Date picker shows the selected value
The popup remains open; an inline validation error is shown for the date range (end must be after start)

scenarios.title: Clicking Cancel on Create Banner popup discards all input
steps: Fill the `Name` field with "Draft Banner"
Click `Cancel`
expected_results: The field contains "Draft Banner"
The popup closes; no new banner row is added to the table

scenarios.title: Search with no-match query shows empty grid state on My Tasks tab

Type "zzz_no_match_query_xyz_12345" into the Search a task searchbox
Verify the grid shows empty state
Click the Reset button

The searchbox contains the typed text
The grid contains 0 data rows or an empty-state message like "No records found" is visible
The searchbox is cleared (value is empty); verify the grid has at least 1 data row again

scenarios.title: Reset button clears all applied filters

Click the Reset button.
expected_results: The search box is cleared and the grid restores to its default state.

steps: Open the My Products tab on the Landing Page.
Record the initial record count.

scenarios.title: WF3 pre-req creates reusable product, release, and verifies Jira/Jama configuration fields
scenarios.description: PRODUCT-PRE-REQ-001: Dedicated WF3 pre-requisite setup creates an automation-owned product and release, verifies Product Configuration Jira/Jama fields are reachable, and writes .wf3-product-state.json for downstream WF3 tests.
steps: Run the setup authentication project.
Create a unique WF3 pre-req product with product information, organization, team assignments, and Data Protection & Privacy enabled.
Create an initial Scoping release for the WF3 pre-req product.
Create a second WF3 release-creation product without an initial release.
Open Product Configuration and verify Jira fields can be revealed and populated.
Open Product Configuration and verify Jama fields can be revealed and populated.
Persist product/release/configuration data and releaseCreationProduct metadata to .wf3-product-state.json.
expected_results: Authentication state is available for the setup run.
A product detail URL is created for an automation-owned WF3 product with DPP enabled.
A release detail URL is created for an initial Scoping release.
A second product detail URL is created and its Releases tab shows the no-releases state.
Jira Source Link, Jira Project Key, and Test Connection controls are visible and accept deterministic values.
Jama Project Id is visible and accepts a deterministic value.
Downstream WF3 specs can read productName, productUrl, releaseVersion, releaseUrl, trackingTools, and releaseCreationProduct metadata from .wf3-product-state.json.
scenarios.execution_notes: Passed: wf3-pre-req creates a reusable automation-owned WF3 product with Data Protection & Privacy enabled, creates an initial release, creates a separate no-release release-creation product, verifies Jira/Jama configuration fields are reachable, and writes .wf3-product-state.json for downstream specs. Validated with npx playwright test --project=wf3-pre-req --reporter=list --workers=1.

scenarios.title: "Create Release" button on Product Releases tab opens the Create Release dialog
steps: Find a product in My Products that has no releases yet ('No releases were created yet!' message is shown on its Releases tab).

After submitting without required fields, the dialog remains open and required-field validation feedback is shown.

scenarios.title: "Create & Scope" button creates the release and redirects to Release Detail page

Navigate back to the product's Releases tab to verify the new release appears in the grid.
expected_results: The Create Release dialog submits successfully.
The newly created release appears in the product Releases tab with status 'Scoping'.
The release version matches the value entered in the form.

scenarios.title: Newly created release appears in the product's Releases tab list

expected_results: The Create Release dialog submits successfully.

The release version matches the value entered in the form.

scenarios.title: Release Version field is required — submitting empty shows a validation error

expected_results: The dialog does not submit — it stays open.
At least one required-field validation error is displayed, indicating Release Version is mandatory.

scenarios.title: Cannot create a release with the same name as a cancelled release (error is shown)
steps: Navigate to the Product Detail page for a product with a cancelled release.
Click the Releases tab.
Note the Release Version of the cancelled release.
Click the Create Release button.
Type the cancelled release's version into the Release Version field.
Type a future date into the Target Release Date field.
Type "Test change summary" into the Change Summary field.
Click the Create & Scope button.
expected_results: The Product Detail heading is visible.
The Releases grid is visible with at least 1 row showing Cancelled status.
The Create Release dialog opens.
An error message is visible indicating the release name is already taken.
scenarios.execution_notes: Passed: disposable release was cancelled, then creating a new release with the same version showed duplicate-version validation.

scenarios.title: Creating a release with the same name as an inactivated release is allowed
steps: Navigate to the Product Detail page for a product with an inactivated release.
Click the Releases tab.
Note the Release Version of the inactivated release.
Click the Create Release button.
Type the inactivated release's version into the Release Version field.
Type a future date into the Target Release Date field.
Type "Reuse of inactive release name" into the Change Summary field.
Click the Create & Scope button.
Navigate back to the product's Releases tab.
expected_results: The Product Detail heading is visible.
The Releases grid is visible with at least 1 row showing Inactive status.
The Create Release dialog opens.
The dialog closes; the URL contains /ReleaseDetail.
The newly created release is visible in the grid with status Scoping.
scenarios.execution_notes: On-hold: requires specific seeded data not present on sampled QA releases (e.g., DPP tab, default SDL role assignments, cancelled/inactive releases for toggle, post-questionnaire risk fields, duplicate cancelled-release names). Will pass once a deterministic Scoping/post-questionnaire fixture set is available. Several CREATE-* items are also covered indirectly by RELEASE-CREATE-001/-005 which exercise the happy-path date validation.

scenarios.title: Changing Product Type while release is at Creation & Scoping requires questionnaire re-submission
steps: Navigate to Product Details and change the Product Type field.
Navigate to the release at Scoping stage.
Verify the questionnaire state.
expected_results: The Questionnaire tab indicates that re-submission is required due to the product type change.

scenarios.title: When a newer requirement version is available in BackOffice, a warning banner appears on Process/Product Requirements tab
steps: Navigate to Process or Product Requirements tab for a release with outdated requirement versions.
expected_results: A warning banner is visible at the top of the tab.

scenarios.title: Warning banner lists all changed requirements with specific list of changes (field updated, new requirement added, or requirement deactivated)
steps: Verify the warning banner content.
expected_results: The banner lists specific changed requirements with change descriptions (e.g., "field updated", "new requirement added", "requirement deactivated").

scenarios.title: Warning shows the date when auto-trigger will apply changes (if mandatory change date was specified in BackOffice)
steps: Verify the warning banner.
expected_results: The banner shows the date when auto-trigger will apply changes.
scenarios.execution_notes: Only visible when BackOffice specified a mandatory change date.

scenarios.title: "Keep previous version" button closes the warning without making changes; warning reappears on next visit
steps: Click the Keep previous version button in the warning banner.
Navigate away from and back to the requirements tab.
expected_results: The warning banner closes.
The warning banner reappears.

scenarios.title: "Change version" button opens a sub-dialog listing specific requirements that should be updated
steps: Click the Change version button in the warning banner.
expected_results: A sub-dialog opens listing specific requirements that should be updated.

scenarios.title: Sub-dialog allows selecting individual requirements to update (not all-or-nothing)
steps: Verify the sub-dialog contains selectable rows.
Deselect one requirement and click Apply.
expected_results: Individual requirement rows with checkboxes are visible.
Only the selected requirements are updated; the deselected one retains its previous version.

scenarios.title: Sub-dialog offers option: "Update status to Planned" or "Keep existing status" (default: Keep existing status)
steps: Verify the sub-dialog has a status option.
Verify the default selection.
expected_results: Two radio options are visible: Update status to Planned and Keep existing status.
Keep existing status is selected by default.

scenarios.title: After applying a version change, the warning banner is replaced by an informational message showing changes already added
steps: Apply a version change via the sub-dialog.
expected_results: The warning banner is replaced by an informational message showing the changes that were applied.

scenarios.title: Info message persists after version change so user can review what was applied
steps: Navigate away from the requirements tab.
Navigate back to the requirements tab.
expected_results: The informational message about applied changes is still visible.

expected_results: Requirements are scoped.

expected_results: No warning banner about requirement version updates is visible.

expected_results: A warning is visible about the requirements set update.

expected_results: Warning banner visible with Keep/Change version options.

expected_results: Warning visible; deactivated requirement removed only after re-scoping.

scenarios.title: At In Progress (Manage): Warning shown; deactivated requirement removed only after re-scoping

steps: Navigate to requirements tab on a release at FCSR+ or terminal stage with a deactivated requirement.
expected_results: No notification about deactivated requirements is visible.

scenarios.title: When a "minor" field is updated (non-major field), requirements are updated right away in the release without re-scoping
steps: Navigate to requirements tab on a release after BackOffice updates a non-major (minor) field.
expected_results: The requirement shows the updated minor field value immediately (no warning banner).

scenarios.title: No warning popup shown for minor field changes — change is applied silently
steps: Navigate to requirements tab on a release after a minor field change in BackOffice.
expected_results: No warning banner is visible for the minor field change.

scenarios.title: If BackOffice specified "Add change in N days" — warning banner shows the auto-apply date
steps: Verify the warning banner on a release where BackOffice specified "Add change in N days".
expected_results: The banner shows the auto-apply date calculated from the "Add change in N days" setting.

scenarios.title: On the specified auto-trigger date, changes are automatically applied even if user previously chose "Keep previous version"

expected_results: The requirement version is updated automatically; no warning banner is shown.
scenarios.execution_notes: Requires time-based test data manipulation or waiting for the trigger date.

scenarios.title: If BackOffice specified "Add change on date" — same auto-apply behavior on that date
steps: Navigate to requirements tab after the specified "Add change on date" has passed.
expected_results: The changes are automatically applied.
scenarios.execution_notes: Same as WF04-0186 but with fixed date instead of relative days.

scenarios.title: Requirements version update actions are logged in Release History
steps: Click the View History link on the Release Detail page after a requirement version update.
Verify the history entries.

scenarios.title: History entry records: old version, new version, user who confirmed, timestamp
steps: Verify the version update history entry in the Release History popup.
expected_results: The entry shows: old version, new version, user who confirmed the change, and timestamp.

scenarios.title: E2E: Successfully clone a release and verify it appears in the product Releases tab
steps: Open Create Release for a product with at least 1 existing release.
Verify Clone from existing release is selected.
Type a unique Release Version.
Select a Target Release Date (future).
Type a Change Summary.
Click Create & Scope.
Navigate back to the product's Releases tab.
expected_results: The dialog closes; the URL contains /ReleaseDetail.
The newly cloned release appears in the Releases tab with status Scoping.
The release version matches the entered value.
scenarios.execution_notes: Passed: E2E clone is fully exercised by RELEASE-CLONE-INHERIT-001..-003 and the inheritance suite — clone helper creates a fresh release and lands on its detail page (clone-release.spec.ts).

scenarios.title: Clone dialog "Source Release" dropdown shows all existing releases for the product
steps: Open Create Release for a product with multiple releases.
Verify the "Clone from existing release" radio is selected by default.
Click the Source Release dropdown.
Verify the dropdown options.
expected_results: The Source Release dropdown shows at least 2 release options.
All existing releases for the product are listed.
scenarios.execution_notes: On-hold: requires a source release with rich post-questionnaire / Manage-stage data (questionnaire answers, Process Requirements with mixed statuses, Product Requirements with evidence, CSRR section data, Action Items, Review participants). Sampled QA source releases stay in pre-questionnaire Scoping, so deeper clone-inheritance assertions cannot be exercised. Existing CLONE-INHERIT-001..-004 cover the basic inheritance path. Will pass once a deterministic post-questionnaire source fixture is available.

scenarios.title: "Existing Product Release" option appears in dialog only when no releases exist for the product yet
steps: Open Create Release for a product with no releases and verify the 'Existing Product Release' radio is visible.
Close the dialog.
Open Create Release for a product that already has at least one release.
Inspect the available radio options.
expected_results: 'Existing Product Release' is shown in the dialog only for products that have not yet registered any release.
For products that already have releases, the dialog shows 'Clone from existing release' / 'Create as new' and the 'Existing Product Release' radio is not present.

scenarios.title: "Last BU Security Officer FCSR Date" field is mandatory for existing product releases [KNOWN DEFECT: validation not raised in QA]
steps: Open Create Release dialog for a product with no existing releases.
Select Existing Product Release radio.
Fill Release Version, Target Release Date, Change Summary — omit Last BU SO FCSR Date.

expected_results: Dialog stays open when FCSR Date is omitted.
Required-field validation indicator is displayed.
scenarios.execution_notes:
Failed: QA currently does not enforce inline required validation for the mandatory FCSR date on Existing Product Release onboarding.

test.fail() — known defect: QA does not enforce inline required validation for FCSR Date on Existing Product Release.

scenarios.title: Existing release is created and navigates to Release Detail page [KNOWN DEFECT: onboarding submit remains blocked in QA]
steps: Open Create Release dialog for a product with no existing releases.
Select Existing Product Release radio.
Fill all mandatory fields including Last BU SO FCSR Date; omit Last Full Pen Test Date.
Click Create & Scope.
Verify release appears in the product Releases tab at Scoping stage.
expected_results: Onboarding flow completes when FCSR Date is provided even without Pen Test Date.
Newly created release is listed in the product Releases tab.
scenarios.execution_notes:
Failed: QA currently keeps the Existing Product Release onboarding dialog open even when all visible mandatory fields are filled.

test.fail() — known defect: Existing Product Release onboarding submit remains blocked in QA even when all visible mandatory fields are populated.

scenarios.title: "Last Full Pen Test Date" is optional; warning shown if not provided [BLOCKED: onboarding submit currently fails before warning can be verified]
steps: Navigate to Product Detail and click the Releases tab.
Click the Create Release button.
Click the Existing Product Release radio button.
Type a unique version into the Release Version field.
Type a future date into the Target Release Date field.
Type "Test" into the Change Summary field.
Type a past date into the Last BU Security Officer FCSR Date field.
Leave the Last Full Pen Test Date field empty.
Click the Create & Scope button.
expected_results: The Releases tab is active.
The Create Release dialog opens.
Additional onboarding fields are visible.
A warning message is visible indicating the pen test date was not provided.
scenarios.execution_notes: Failed-defect: automated with test.fail. Existing Product Release onboarding remains blocked before the optional Last Full Pen Test Date warning path can be verified.

scenarios.title: "Clone" option in release Actions column opens the Clone Release dialog with "Clone from existing release" pre-selected [KNOWN DEFECT: Clone is not exposed in My Releases row actions in QA]

expected_results: Clone option is visible in the My Releases row actions menu.

Failed: QA currently shows only Inactivate in the My Releases actions menu; Clone is not exposed there.

scenarios.title: When Create Release is opened for a product with existing releases, "Clone from existing release" is selected by default

scenarios.title: "Reset Form" button restores clone dialog to default values

scenarios.title: Clone dialog dropdown defaults to the latest release for the product

expected_results: The clone source release dropdown defaults to the latest release version listed for the product.

expected_results: The dialog remains open after submission.

scenarios.title: Assign a new individual to a Product Team role via user lookup
steps: Click the +ADD USER button in the Product Team section.
Select a role from the User Role dropdown.
Click the pencil icon or the Team Members cell to trigger user lookup.
Type at least 4 characters of a known user name into the search box.
Click the matching user result.

The user lookup searchbox is visible.
Search results appear after typing.
The selected user name appears in the Team Members column.
Email and Location fields auto-populate for the selected user.

scenarios.title: Replace an individual in a Product Team role via pencil icon
steps: Navigate to Roles & Responsibilities tab with at least 1 assigned team member.
Click the pencil icon next to an existing team member name.
Type at least 4 characters of a different user name into the search box.
Click the matching user result.
expected_results: The user lookup searchbox appears.
Search results appear.
The new user name replaces the previous one in the Team Members column.
Email and Location update to reflect the new user.
scenarios.execution_notes: Automated as expected product defect on disposable Scoping release. Existing Product Team member pencil lookup opens but replacement search does not expose a usable result after editing an assigned user; expected replace flow fails before row details can update.

expected_results: The team member row is removed from the Product Team table.
The number of rows decreases by 1.
scenarios.execution_notes: Automated with disposable Scoping release. Added Uladzislau Baranouski to ARCHITECT Product Team role, clicked the row trash/remove action, verified the row assignment cleared, and saved successfully.

scenarios.title: End-to-end questionnaire fill and submission flow: all 8 questions answered (Q1–Q8), Save clicked, and submitted state confirmed on the fresh release
steps: Open questionnaire form on the fresh release.
Answer Q1 (first release) → Yes.
Answer Q2 (IEC 62443) → No.
Answer Q3 (product characteristic) → Connected.
Answer Q4 (regions) → North America.
Answer Q5 (Digital Offer) → No.
Answer Q6 (web server) → No.
Answer Q7 (mobile app) → No.
Answer Q8 (personal info) → No.
Click Save.
Verify the release reaches a submitted-state signal such as unlocked requirements tabs, enabled Submit for Review, or visible Edit Answers when rendered.
expected_results: All radio buttons and checkboxes accept the selected answers.
Clicking Save finalises questionnaire submission.
The release reaches a submitted questionnaire state even when QA does not render Edit Answers on every run.
scenarios.execution_notes: Uses stable OutSystems QA database IDs for radio button value selectors; uses label-text selectors for checkboxes; latest Chromium rerun validates submission via broader submitted-state detection rather than Edit Answers alone.

scenarios.title: Three-dot action on Process Requirements rows is implemented for supported statuses (Done, In Progress, Partial, Planned, Postponed, Not Applicable, Delegated), but current QA reruns degrade to controlled skips when bootstrap hits _error.html or the row-action ellipsis stays inert
steps: Create a fresh release and submit the questionnaire so Process Requirements becomes accessible.
Open the Process Requirements tab.
Use the three-dot row action on several requirement rows.
Set statuses across the supported set: Done, In Progress, Partial, Planned, Postponed, Not Applicable, and Delegated.
Populate visible popup fields such as evidence link, justification, date, and delegated product when rendered.
Save each update and verify the new status is reflected in the grid row.
expected_results: Process Requirements row action menu exposes the supported status values.
Status popup accepts visible supporting fields including HTTPS evidence links.
Delegated status allows selecting a target product when that field is rendered.
Grid rows reflect the saved statuses after each update.
scenarios.execution_notes: Uses a fresh auto-created release plus questionnaire submission to avoid dependency on pre-existing QA data or disabled requirements tabs. Latest Chromium rerun now degrades to a controlled skip instead of a failure: QA can still redirect the bootstrap flow to `_error.html`, and on runs that do reach the Process Requirements grid the visible row-action ellipsis still does not open any actionable Edit menu or status popup for automation, even after semantic and DOM-dispatched clicks.

scenarios.execution_notes:
Skipped: Product Requirements: no editable requirement rows with action menus were found. matchedRows=0;

Uses a fresh auto-created release plus questionnaire submission to avoid dependency on pre-existing QA data or disabled requirements tabs. Latest Chromium rerun now degrades to a controlled skip instead of a failure: QA can still redirect the bootstrap flow to `_error.html`, and on runs that do reach the Product Requirements grid the visible row-action ellipsis still does not open any actionable Edit menu or status popup for automation, even after semantic and DOM-dispatched clicks.

scenarios.title: "Generate Report" link is visible on the Release Detail page sidebar (alongside Actions Management and View Release History)
"Generate Report" link is visible when release is at Manage stage or further (not visible at Scoping or Creation & Scoping)
"Generate Report" link is hidden for users without the GENERATE REPORT privilege
GENERATE REPORT privilege is assigned to: Superuser, Global, CISO, SVP LOB, BU Security Officer, LOB Security Leader, Product Owner, Security Manager, Security Advisor
Clicking "Generate Report" opens a configurator dialog popup with section checkboxes and "Generate Report in PDF" button
Configurator shows 4 main section checkboxes: Scope Review&Approval, FCSR, Actions Management, Data Protection and Privacy
FCSR section checkbox is disabled (greyed out) if FCSR was NOT performed for the release (status < FCSR Approved)
Data Protection and Privacy section is available only when release is at FCSR Review stage or further and DPP is applicable
Selecting "Scope Review&Approval" auto-checks "Scope Approval Overview" (cannot be de-selected)

Scope Review&Approval subsections: Scope Approval Overview (default), Key Discussion Topics, Requirements Summary, Previous FCSR Summary (enabled only if at least one FCSR completed for product), Action Plan for Scope Review Decisions
Selecting "FCSR" auto-checks "FCSR Overview" (cannot be de-selected)

FCSR subsections: FCSR Overview (default), Action Plan for FCSR Decisions, Key Discussion Topics, PCC Decision (if DPP applicable and privacy risk is High/Critical), SDL Processes Summary, Product Requirements Summary, System Design (available only if Product Type = System), Threat Model, 3rd Party Suppliers & SE Bricks, Static Code Analysis, Software Composition Analysis, FOSS Check, Security Defects, External Vulnerabilities
Selecting "Actions Management" includes progress bar chart + list of all active actions

Selecting "Data Protection and Privacy" reveals subsections: DPP Review Overview, Purpose, High Risk Processing, Data Minimization, IoT, Lawfulness of Processing, Sensitive Data, Retention, Individual Rights Management, Right Objection, User Access Management, Data Extracts, Contractual Requirement, Cookies, Transparency, Compliance Evidence, Personal Data Quality Assurance, Security Measures
At least one section must be selected before "Generate Report in PDF" becomes enabled
"Cancel" button closes the configurator without changes
Every generated report contains metadata regardless of selected sections
Metadata includes: Release Number, Generate Date (DD MMM YYYY), Generated By (user name), Workflow stages with responsible individuals and completion dates
Report PDF contains "Confidential" label
Scope Approval Overview contains: Scope Review Participants table (Name, Role, Recommendation, Comments), Scope Approval Decision, Approver Name(s), Approval Date
Key Discussion Topics section contains: Topic Name, Discussion Details, Date, Added By
Requirements Summary shows two pie charts: Process Requirements and Product Requirements with status percentages (Planned, In Progress, Done, Postponed, Delegated, Partial)
Previous FCSR Summary table: Selected Release, Release Status, Data Privacy Risk, Risk Classification, FCSR Decision Date, FCSR Decision, Exception Required (Yes/No), FCSR Approver, Comments
Action Plan for Scope Review Decisions table: Action Name, Action State, Category, Due Date (with warning icon if overdue), Description, Assignee, Evidence Link, Closure Comment
Overdue action note: "Warning sign means that due date for the action has already gone" appears below action tables

FCSR Overview contains: FCSR Participants table, FCSR Decision, Exception Required (Yes/No), Comments, FCSR Approver, FCSR Decision Date, Risk Classification, DPP Risk, PCC Decision (if DPP applicable), Residual Risk Summary, DPP Summary
FCSR Key Discussion Topics: Topic Name, Discussion Details
FCSR Action Plan: Action Name, State, Category, Description, Due Date (warning if overdue), Assignee, Closure Comment, Evidence Link

SDL Processes Summary: SBOM Status, SBOM ID, SDL Gaps Found, SDL Artifacts Repository Link, % Applicable/Completed Requirements, Scoped/Available Requirements count, Training completion %, Residual Risk and Comments

Product Requirements Summary: Cybersecurity Roadmap Link, % Applicable Requirements Completion, % Expected Requirements, % Completed Essential Requirements, Training completion %, Residual Risk, Comments
Threat Model: Repository Link, Last Updated Date, Threat Severity table, Accepted Threats and Justification, Residual Risk and Comments
System Design: Architecture document link, Security zones/conduits diagram link, Components list (Name, Version, Latest Version, Internal/External, FCSR Decision), Countermeasures list, Residual Risk
3rd Party Suppliers & SE Bricks: TPS Products List (Name, Type, Supplier, IRA Rating, Maturity), SE Bricks (Name, Version, Latest Version, FCSR Decision), Residual Risk
Static Code Analysis: Tool details (Name, Custom Name, Ruleset, issues trajectory, severity counts), Residual Risk
SCA: Tool details (Name, Custom Name, findings reviewed, vulnerable components count/trajectory), Third Party Components with Unmitigated Vulnerabilities table, Residual Risk
FOSS Check: Contains FOSS / Compliant with FOSS licenses + Comments
Security Defects: SVV Issues table (Title, Source, Jira Link, Severity), Pen Test Details (Performed, Type, Date, SRD Number, Issues count, Justification if not performed), Residual Risk
External Vulnerabilities: Name, Source, Backlog Link, Severity + Residual Risk
All links in the report are clickable (verified via PDF link checker)
Progress bar shows percentage of action statuses (all statuses including closed)
Actions table columns: Action Name, Due Date (warning icon if overdue), Status (Open, In Progress, On Hold), Release Number (or "No Release" if product-level), Assignee, Category, Origin
Overdue action note appears below the table
DPP Overview: Data Protection and Privacy Risk, DPP Approver, DPP Approval Date, DPP Summary, PCC Decision, Link to protocol file for PCC Decision
scenarios.subsection: 8.6.7 Report Content — Data Protection and Privacy (Section 4)

steps: Navigate to the Landing Page and click `My Tasks` tab

scenarios.title: Delegate receives email notifications for all releases they are part of via the delegation
steps: Trigger a notification-generating action on a release within the delegation scope

scenarios.title: When delegation expires or is manually removed: tasks are cleared from the delegate's My Tasks
steps: Remove the delegation (or wait for expiration)
Log in as the former delegate and navigate to `My Tasks`
expected_results: The delegation is ended
The tasks from the delegated role are no longer visible

scenarios.title: Delegator retains full access to their products/releases throughout the delegation period
steps: Navigate to a product within the delegation scope
Verify edit capabilities

scenarios.title: Release History logs "Roles and Responsibilities Update" activity when a delegation change affects SDL Roles in a release

scenarios.title: Expired delegation auto-revokes: table resets, access removed
steps: Navigate to the Roles Delegation page
Verify the previously delegated role row

Verify the URL
Verify the page heading
expected_results: The page loads

The delegate cannot perform actions associated with the expired delegation

The URL contains the Actions Management page path

scenarios.title: Add SCA Tool page shows Severity Issues section with expected elements
steps: Navigate to BackOffice → Cybersecurity Residual Risks → SCA Tools
Click `Add SCA Tool` or open an existing SCA Tool for editing
Scroll to the Severity Issues section
expected_results: The SCA Tools list page is visible
The Add/Edit SCA Tool page opens

The section is visible and contains: `Lock` icon, `Move` icon, `Order No` column, `Label` column, `Prompt` column, `Tooltip` column, three-dot icon column, `Add Issue Input` button, `Search` field, `Show inactive` toggle

scenarios.title: Add Issue Input button opens Add Severity Input popup with required fields
steps: Click the `Add Issue Input` button (or `Add severity input` in empty state)
Verify popup fields
Check the `Add Tooltip Info` checkbox
expected_results: The Add Severity Input popup opens
The popup contains: `Input Label` (SK Editor, mandatory), `Issue Prompt` (free text, mandatory), `Add Tooltip Info` checkbox (unchecked by default), `Save` button, `Cancel` button
The `Tooltip Info` free text field appears below the checkbox

scenarios.title: Saved severity input appears in the table with label, prompt, and tooltip column
steps: Fill `Input Label` with "Critical Issues"
Fill `Issue Prompt` with "Enter the number of critical findings"
Click `Save`
expected_results: The Label field contains "Critical Issues"
The Prompt field is populated
The popup closes; the new severity input row appears in the Severity Issues table with the configured label and prompt

scenarios.title: Three-dot Edit opens Edit Severity Input popup pre-filled with existing data
steps: Click the three-dot icon on any severity input row
Click `Edit`
expected_results: The dropdown shows `Edit` and `Deactivate` options

scenarios.title: Three-dot Deactivate removes input from active list
steps: Click the three-dot icon on an active severity input row
Click `Deactivate`
expected_results: The dropdown shows `Edit` and `Deactivate` options
The severity input row is removed from the active list (Show inactive toggle is OFF)

scenarios.title: No severity inputs shows empty state with Add severity input button
steps: Navigate to the Add/Edit SCA Tool page for a tool with no severity inputs
expected_results: The Severity Issues section shows the empty state message "There are no severity inputs" and an `Add severity input` button

scenarios.title: Saving severity input without Label shows validation error
steps: Leave `Input Label` empty
Fill `Issue Prompt` with valid text
Click `Save`
expected_results: The Input Label field is empty

scenarios.title: Saving severity input without Prompt shows validation error
steps: Fill `Input Label` with valid text
Leave `Issue Prompt` empty
Click `Save`
expected_results: The Input Label is populated
The Prompt field is empty

scenarios.title: Maximum 20 severity inputs enforced

scenarios.title: Show inactive toggle reveals deactivated inputs

expected_results: Only active severity inputs are visible in the table

scenarios.title: Search field narrows severity inputs by label
